title: error running container: from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c mkdir -p /config]: mount `/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/d67e7e4f18895db83e729e8321e0c9f791f5c1407495c8c8dc5e494da5997798/userdata/overlay/3392401135/merge` to `/tmp`: Operation not permitted[Bug]:
body:
### Issue Description

Team,

I have below issue when i try to create the image with podman (version 4.3.1) and any help is appreciated.

Dockerfile:

FROM docker.io/openjdk:17-jdk-alpine
VOLUME /tmp
EXPOSE 9440
RUN mkdir -p /config
ADD keystore.jks /config/keystore.jks
ADD target/SampleWebService-0.1.jar SampleWebService-0.1.jar
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "java -jar SampleWebService-0.1.jar"]

Error:
error running container: from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c mkdir -p /config]: mount `/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/d67e7e4f18895db83e729e8321e0c9f791f5c1407495c8c8dc5e494da5997798/userdata/overlay/3392401135/merge` to `/tmp`: Operation not permitted

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman -t samplewebserice:1.0 .
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

STEP 1/7: FROM docker.io/openjdk:17-jdk-alpine
STEP 2/7: VOLUME /tmp
--> Using cache 0501cef2a945febf31e82f0578d35332918868a05c91d51ae46729b3b8d1ba13
--> 0501cef2a94
STEP 3/7: EXPOSE 9440
--> Using cache 60f2f069be6c56516f2446d0e13c36589921cfeb642df3800304e472649f61eb
--> 60f2f069be6
STEP 4/7: RUN mkdir -p /config
error running container: from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c mkdir -p /config]: mount `/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/d67e7e4f18895db83e729e8321e0c9f791f5c1407495c8c8dc5e494da5997798/userdata/overlay/3392401135/merge` to `/tmp`: Operation not permitted
: exit status 1
Error: building at STEP "RUN mkdir -p /config": while running runtime: exit status 1

### Describe the results you expected

Creating imaged successfully

### podman info output

```shell
podman Desktop 4.3.1 running in windows 10
```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: can anyone assign this issue to me. I wanna work upon this...
Hey @Luap99, I think the issue is resolved by the user itself, so you can close this issue:)
